description_long: This is a vintage-style record player housed in a rectangular wooden cabinet with tapered legs and a hinged lid. The lid, when open, reveals the word 'JAZZ' in bold, decorative lettering on its inner surface, surrounded by ornate corner designs. The top surface features a turntable platter with a vinyl record, a tonearm, and a set of control knobs and buttons, all set into a contrasting cream panel. The front of the cabinet displays a woven speaker grille with two large circular speaker outlines and a central metallic latch. The sides and back are finished in a wood grain texture, with the back panel being plain and unadorned except for a small port. The overall design evokes mid-century aesthetics, blending functionality with retro charm.
description: A vintage wooden record player with a hinged lid labeled 'JAZZ', featuring a turntable, control knobs, and a woven speaker grille. The cabinet stands on tapered legs and has a retro mid-century design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid with 'JAZZ' lettering, the turntable, control panel, and prominent woven speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the cabinet, the open lid, and the side profile of the turntable and controls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wood grain back panel and lower beige section with a small port, no visible controls or speakers.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the open lid, the turntable, and the control panel from the opposite side, mirroring the left profile.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and centrally located on the top surface, appearing as a circular black disk with a blue and white vinyl record on it. It is moderately sized relative to the cabinet, with a smooth, matte finish and visible grooves on the record. The platter is set into a cream-colored panel, contrasting with the darker wood.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending from the right side of the turntable towards the center of the platter. It is slender, metallic, and has a black finish with a visible joint near the base. The tonearm is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready to play.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small, pointed component at the end of the tonearm, appearing metallic and distinct from the rest of the arm. It is attached to the tonearm and positioned above the record.
attribute_control_knobs: There are four control knobs and one red button located on the right side of the top panel. The knobs are round, black, and of medium size, while the button is larger and red. They are set into a cream-colored section, with no visible labels or markings.
attribute_dust_cover: A dust cover is present in the form of a hinged wooden lid, which is currently open. The lid is opaque, matching the wood grain of the cabinet, and is attached with visible hinges at the back. It does not appear to be transparent or removable.
